title: Need/Requirement Item
description: A minimal-core, extensible schema for user needs (the why) and requirements (the what/how much), with graph interrelations. Aligned v2: relations now carry target_kind, a shared 'classification' facet block was added, and summary/source vocabularies were unified with the other content types.

kind.description: Whether this item expresses a user/stakeholder need (the why) or a requirement (the what/how much).

statement.description: The full need or requirement text. Supports Markdown.

rationale.description: Why this item is worded/scoped as it is - the justification, trade-off, or constraint behind the current statement.

classification.description: Shared subject facets so needs appear in the same filtered views as use cases and case studies, and so a need can be tied to a market segment and the products that could serve it.

attributes.description: Open bag of type-specific, structured spec key-values. Typical keys for an information need: topic, geographic_scope, temporal_scope, update_frequency, format_preference, urgency, audience_expertise. Typical keys for an observational requirement: parameter, spatial_resolution, temporal_resolution, accuracy, coverage_area, latency, data_type, verification_method. (Distinct from 'classification', which is for cross-type faceting.)

tags.description: Free-text labels for filtering/grouping, independent of structured attributes and classification.

relations.description: Typed links to other graph items. Internal RE edges (derives_from, part_of, consolidates, conflicts_with, duplicates, depends_on, supersedes) plus 'satisfied_by' to point at a use case or external artifact that fulfils this item.
